title: Particle Forge
description: A GPU particle-system editor — wire up emitters and force fields in 3D, tune 5 presets, and export a WebM or preset file.
concept: A real-time WebGL particle-system builder rendered as glowing point sprites in an orbitable 3D scene. Configure up to 6 independent emitters (point/cone/ring/box/burst shapes) and four global force fields (gravity, wind, vortex, curl-noise turbulence), then watch Fire, Snow, Fireworks, Galaxy and Smoke come alive as fully-authored presets you can remix.
features: 6 independent emitters across 5 shapes (point, cone, ring, box, one-shot burst) with per-emitter color/size/rate/speed/spread controls
4 tunable global force fields: gravity, wind, orbital vortex, and curl-noise turbulence
5 built-in presets (Fire, Snow, Fireworks, Galaxy, Smoke) plus save-your-own custom presets to localStorage
Orbit + pinch-zoom 3D camera with auto-rotate, built on a hand-rolled perspective/lookAt WebGL pipeline
One-click PNG snapshot and WebM video recording export (MediaRecorder + canvas.captureStream)
Export/import full scene configs as JSON, with autosave/restore of the last session via localStorage
tech: Structure-of-arrays particle pool with swap-remove recycling

interaction.mouse: Drag to orbit the camera, scroll wheel to zoom
interaction.click: Pick a preset from the bottom bar; use the right panel to add/edit emitters and force fields
interaction.touch: One-finger drag to orbit, two-finger pinch to zoom, tap any control in the panel
interaction.keyboard: Space toggles camera auto-rotate, R resets the camera to the preset's default view
